skill.name: responsive-design
skill.description: Create responsive web designs that work across all devices and screen sizes. Use when building mobile-first layouts, implementing breakpoints, or optimizing for different viewports. Handles CSS Grid, Flexbox, media queries, viewport units, and responsive images.
skill.summary: Create responsive web layouts using mobile-first design with CSS Grid, Flexbox, and media queries.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate documentation resource for responsive web design. The static scanner produced false positives by misidentifying CSS code examples, markdown links, and reference URLs as security vulnerabilities. All 86 detected patterns are false positives - the skill contains only documentation and CSS/HTML code examples with no executable code, network calls to external services, or file system operations beyond standard documentation references.

low_findings.title: CSS Code Examples Misidentified as External Commands
low_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged CSS syntax (`.container`, `@media`, etc.) as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are legitimate CSS code blocks in documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct code inspection confirms these are CSS code blocks, not executable shell commands. The scanner pattern matching is incorrectly triggering on CSS syntax.

low_findings.title: Documentation Reference URLs Misidentified as Network Security Issue
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged reference URLs (MDN, CSS-Tricks) as hardcoded URLs. These are legitimate documentation links in the References section.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are standard documentation reference links, not API endpoints or exfiltration URLs.

low_findings.title: Markdown Relative Links Misidentified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged `../ui-component-patterns/SKILL.md` as path traversal. These are standard markdown relative links to related skills.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are documentation cross-references, not file system access operations.

content.value_statement: Create websites that adapt beautifully to any screen size using mobile-first design principles, CSS Grid, Flexbox, and modern responsive techniques.
actual_capabilities: Create mobile-first responsive layouts using CSS Grid and Flexbox
Implement responsive images with srcset, picture element, and image-set
Build fluid typography using clamp() and viewport units
Use container queries for component-based responsive design
Apply breakpoints and responsive best practices
limitations: Does not generate complete website code from scratch
Does not provide design assets or images
Does not test across actual devices or browsers
Does not optimize images or generate image variants

use_cases.title: New Website Development
use_cases.description: Build responsive layouts for new web applications using mobile-first approach with CSS Grid and Flexbox.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers building new web projects

use_cases.title: Legacy Site Modernization
use_cases.description: Convert fixed-width layouts to responsive designs that work on all devices.

use_cases.title: Component Library Creation
use_cases.description: Create reusable responsive components using container queries for modular design systems.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Responsive Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a responsive layout using mobile-first CSS with a container, grid, and breakpoint at 768px. Show how the layout changes from mobile to tablet.

prompt_templates.title: Responsive Navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a responsive navigation component with a hamburger menu on mobile that expands to a horizontal menu on tablet and desktop.

prompt_templates.title: Responsive Image Gallery
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a responsive image gallery that shows 1 column on mobile, 2 on tablet, and 4 on desktop using CSS Grid.

prompt_templates.title: Container Query Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a card component that adapts its layout based on its container size using CSS container queries.

output_examples.input: Create a mobile-first grid layout with cards
output_examples.output: CSS code showing a grid that starts as 1 column, becomes 2 columns at 640px, and 3 columns at 1024px, with fluid gap and padding using rem units.

output_examples.input: Make typography responsive without media queries
output_examples.output: CSS using clamp() function for fluid font sizes that scale smoothly between minimum and maximum values based on viewport width.

best_practices: Always start with mobile styles as the default, then add larger breakpoints
Use relative units (rem, em, %, vw/vh) instead of fixed pixels for scalability
Use CSS Grid for 2-dimensional layouts and Flexbox for 1-dimensional layouts
anti_patterns: Avoid fixed widths like width: 1200px - use max-width instead
Do not duplicate styles across all breakpoints - use mobile-first inheritance
Avoid using max-width media queries for the base styles - this creates desktop-first code

faq.question: What is mobile-first design?
faq.answer: Mobile-first design means starting with styles for the smallest screens as the default, then adding larger breakpoints with min-width media queries. This approach ensures the core experience works on all devices.

faq.question: When should I use CSS Grid vs Flexbox?
faq.answer: Use CSS Grid for 2-dimensional layouts (rows and columns together). Use Flexbox for 1-dimensional layouts (either a row OR a column). Grid is better for overall page layout, Flexbox for component-level alignment.

faq.question: What are container queries?
faq.answer: Container queries allow you to style elements based on their parent container size rather than the viewport size. This enables truly reusable responsive components.

faq.question: How do I make images responsive?
faq.answer: Use the srcset attribute to provide multiple image sizes, the picture element for art direction (different images at different sizes), and CSS background-image with image-set for high-DPI displays.

faq.question: What are the standard breakpoints?
faq.answer: Common breakpoints are: Mobile (320-767px), Tablet (768-1023px), Desktop (1024-1439px), and Large screens (1440px+). Adjust these based on your content needs.

faq.question: How do I make typography responsive?
faq.answer: Use the CSS clamp() function for fluid typography that scales between minimum and maximum values. Example: font-size: clamp(1rem, 2.5vw, 1.5rem) scales smoothly with viewport width.
